danger_signals.visual_form: Pointed metallic obstacles scattered in level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Instant damage on contact; one-hit kill if no rings collected

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark void below walkable surfaces, often with visual separation/drop-off edge
danger_signals.meaning: Instant level failure and life loss if fallen into

danger_signals.visual_form: Blue liquid environments in zones like Aquatic Ruin; visual submersion of player
danger_signals.meaning: Drowning timer; player loses air and dies if not surfaced within time limit

danger_signals.visual_form: Mechanical enemies with distinct silhouettes; often colored gray/metallic with red accents or zone-specific coloring
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile entities; collision causes ring loss or death if unprotected

danger_signals.visual_form: Spiraling track sections and platforms that shift position or rotate
danger_signals.meaning: Hazard of momentum loss, collision with edges, or falling if platform timing is missed

danger_signals.visual_form: Timer UI element counting down; visual stress cues when near expiration
danger_signals.meaning: Game over state if time runs out; pressure signal for level progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct visual separation; zone-themed battleground with Robotnik's machine as focal point
danger_signals.meaning: High-danger encounter; Robotnik attacks in themed mechanical form specific to zone

safety_signals.visual_form: Bright yellow/gold circular collectibles distributed throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Health currency; one ring negates one hit; more rings = greater survival buffer

safety_signals.visual_form: Glowing shield icon item; visual aura around player when collected
safety_signals.meaning: One free hit absorbed without ring loss; visual indicator of temporary invulnerability layer

safety_signals.visual_form: Star or glowing effect; player sprite wrapped in temporary visual effect
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability period; enemies cannot damage player; audio/visual cue of active state

safety_signals.visual_form: Sonic head icon or life symbol item box
safety_signals.meaning: Grants additional life; extends play session before game over

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual marker or post in level geometry indicating save point
safety_signals.meaning: Progress save; respawn location if player dies; safety anchor in level progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Coiled spring or launch pad graphics; red/yellow colored mechanical objects
safety_signals.meaning: Momentum boost; propels player to safety or shortcuts; enables high-speed traversal

safety_signals.visual_form: Zone act completion screen; visual transition to next zone theme
safety_signals.meaning: Progress security; advance to new environment; level threat reset

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Sonic the Hedgehog character model; spiky silhouette; red shoes
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: No direct variant; Sonic remains visually consistent across zones
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Natural, organic zone themes (Green Hill, Aquatic Ruin, Hill Top Zone); nature-aligned aesthetics suggest protection of natural world

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Fox character with two tails; can fly (tail propulsion); smaller than Sonic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: None; Tails is allied NPC/co-player in single-player mode
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears alongside Sonic; indicates cooperative gameplay; two-player presence

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Robotnik's flying machine; egg-shaped cockpit; rotund silhouette; goggles and mustache character design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Zone-specific robot armies; mechanical enemies throughout levels reflect Robotnik's industrial/technological faction; metal coloring (gray, red, black)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Industrial, mechanical zones (Hill Top Zone, Chemical Plant Zone, Metropolis Zone); heavy machinery; technological takeover aesthetics; Death Egg space station as faction headquarters

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Small animal sprites within captured robot shells; visible in boss defeats when animals are freed
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemies are animal-based robots (trapped creatures in mechanical suits); freeing animals is visual reward
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Natural zone designs suggest habitats; animal presence in freed state at zone completion indicates restoration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Zone environment displays industrial/mechanical overlay; natural elements suppressed or corrupted; Robotnik's machines visible; zone themed as 'taken over' (e.g., Chemical Plant has toxic coloring)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy robots patrol and attack; hazards are dense; boss machine appears at zone end; animals remain trapped in robot shells

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Zone transitions to clean/restored visual state; mechanical elements fade or are destroyed; animals freed from robot shells; visual transition to next zone or completion screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies cease spawning; boss defeated and removed from level; Robotnik flees or is shown in retreat animation; freed animals appear on screen

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Chaos Emeralds missing/Robotnik powering Death Egg
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Death Egg space station visible in background or end-game scenes; threatening presence in sky; industrial/apocalyptic tone in later zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Robotnik continues to spawn enemies; zone difficulty increases; end boss encounters escalate; final confrontation at space station

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Special stage access (pre-rendered 3D environments); visual reward sequences showing emerald collection; progress toward goal communicated through HUD
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Special stages triggered at intervals; visual 3D graphics contrast with 2D main gameplay; emerald acquisition displayed on completion screen

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Player low on health/rings near zero
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sonic sprite may show stress animation; visual HUD emphasizes ring count (often displayed prominently); screen may flash or color shift warning
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player moves cautiously; single-hit vulnerability; heightened collision awareness; risk of immediate failure state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Timer UI element flashing red or accelerating visually; visual stress cues (screen shake, color shift); urgency conveyed through HUD design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced player movement toward goal; rushed gameplay; avoidance of exploration; linear progression pressure

environmental_storytelling.example: Early zones (Green Hill, Chemical Plant) represent natural world threatened by Robotnik; later zones (Metropolis, Oil Ocean, Casino Night) show increasing industrialization and environmental corruption; visual progression tells story of invasion

Enemy robot sprites contain visible small animal silhouettes inside mechanical bodies; visual representation of Robotnik's enslavement; freeing animals (on boss defeat) shows player agency in liberation narrative

environmental_storytelling.technique: Death Egg space station as looming threat
environmental_storytelling.example: Large mechanical structure visible in background of later zones and cinematics; grows visually closer as player progresses; represents Robotnik's ultimate goal and end-game threat without explicit text

environmental_storytelling.technique: Zone environmental hazards reflecting faction control
environmental_storytelling.example: Chemical Plant Zone contains toxic liquid and mechanical hazards; Hill Top Zone shows volcanic/industrial corruption; Metropolis Zone displays heavy machinery; visual environment reflects Robotnik's technological dominion
Water in early zones is navigable challenge; in corrupted zones becomes toxic/dangerous; visual color shift (blue → sickly green/orange) communicates environmental degradation

environmental_storytelling.technique: Boss machine design reflecting zone identity
environmental_storytelling.example: Each Robotnik encounter uses zone-specific mechanical design (aquatic-themed in Aquatic Ruin, mine-themed in Hill Top); visual cohesion between environment and threat reinforces world consistency

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard run/jump/curl animations; collision causes ring scatter and audio cue; vulnerability visual feedback
Visual aura around sprite; shield absorbs one hit; shield breaks with visual effect; temporary invulnerability layer
Sprite displays glowing/star effect; rapid color cycling or shimmer animation; enemies cannot damage; audio cue confirms state
Animation shows struggle/panic; sprite colors shift or flash; countdown timer visible; visual urgency cue
Mechanical animation cycles; attack patterns synchronized with visual tells; machine movements telegraph incoming danger; defeat animation shows destruction/retreat
Predictable movement patterns; visual patrol cycles; no immediate aggression animation; collision triggers damage
Explosion animation; sprite destruction visual effect; sometimes reveals trapped animal sprite; visual reward for player
Follows Sonic at offset distance; animation mirrors Sonic's movement; can assist in reaching areas; visual indicator of ally presence
Full independent control; separate animation cycles from Sonic; can separate from main player; distinct visual identity maintained
Visible but immobile within robot body; no independent movement; sprite partially obscured by mechanical frame; visual captivity state
Break free from robot shell animation; run or fly away with joyful animation; visual reward sequence; contributes to zone completion narrative

notes: Sonic 2 uses minimal text-based narrative; world-state communication is primarily visual and behavioral. Danger signals are conveyed through environmental design, enemy presence, and hazard placement rather than explicit warnings. Faction identity is established through color coding (Sonic=blue/natural, Robotnik=red/industrial) and environmental theming. The game's progression from natural to industrial zones visually narrates the story of invasion and takeover. Power-ups and collectibles use bright, distinct visuals for quick recognition during high-speed play. Boss encounters provide zone-specific visual context for Robotnik's faction through mechanical design reflecting zone themes. The freed animals mechanic provides visual reward and narrative satisfaction through liberation imagery.
